title: otholobium foliosum subsp. gazense
intro: Otholobium foliosum subsp. gazense is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae, native to southern Africa. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various health conditions. The leaves and stems of Otholobium foliosum subsp. gazense are rich in antioxidants and have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reduced inflammation and improved immune function. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as infusions and decoctions, to treat fever, coughs, and respiratory issues.
